message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Monterey Crossings (Chick-fil-A, Dickey's Barbecue Pit, Pokehana) (Palm Desert)
Cuisine/type signal: Fast-casual / chain dining cluster
Why it surfaced in discovery: Anchor commercial cluster shaping the north Palm Desert development zone: 18-acre, 130,000 sq ft project at Dinah Shore and Monterey Avenue. First wave of tenants (Chick-fil-A, Dickey's Barbecue Pit, Pokehana) recently opened; a 130-room hotel still in the works.
Discovery sources: https://gpsbusinessinsider.com/prime-palm-desert-development-opportunity/ | https://www.adwarchitects.com/retail-and-restaurant-monterey-shore-plazapalm-desert-ca | https://www.palmspringslife.com/vision/palm-desert-downtown/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Monterey Crossings in Palm Desert:

Monterey Crossing is a retail center under construction at the northeast corner of Dinah Shore and Monterey Avenue in Palm Desert. The 18-acre project includes a 130-room, four-story hotel along with 130,000 square feet of single-story commercial space.

Signed tenants include Chick-Fil-A along with other fast casual dining options such as Dickey's BBQ and Pokehana. A new Chick-fil-A restaurant began serving Palm Desert and the Coachella Valley on July 14, 2022, with its location at 73070 Dinah Shore Drive, Palm Desert, CA, 92211, open for dine-in, drive-thru and mobile drive-thru from 6:30 a.m. to 10 p.m., Monday through Saturday.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Monterey Crossings development in Palm Desert:

## Location and Overview
Monterey Crossings is a new retail center under construction at the northeast corner of Dinah Shore Drive and Monterey Avenue in Palm Desert.

## Project Details
Monterey Crossings is an 18-acre project that will include a 130-room, four-story hotel along with 130,000 square feet of single-story commercial space. The Palm Desert City Council approved the Specific Plan and other documents for the Monterey Crossing project on May 11, 2017.

## Current Status
The 18-acre, 130,000-square-foot Monterey Crossings project has recently seen its first businesses open including Chick-fil-A and other fast casual dining options. A 130-room, four-story hotel is still in the works for the site. Constructed in 2022, the retail pad sits on a 1.18-acre lot at 3120 Dinah Shore Drive, at the hard corner of the signalized intersection of Monterey Avenue and Dinah Shore Drive.

The development represents an important commercial expansion for Palm Desert's north side, featuring various retail, dining, and hospitality components.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Dickey's Barbecue in the Palm Desert/Dinah Shore area:

## Location and Reviews

Reviews mention that food quality is great with generous servings, and staff is always very friendly and nice. One customer noted parking was easy, with a very casual atmosphere featuring a small dining area and huge outdoor space that felt very open.

Positive reviews highlight that the food is delicious with friendly staff, and the restaurant is cute and clean. Another review specifically praised the beef brisket and pulled pork sandwiches as "melt in your mouth great flavor" in a cute place with fun music and excellent service.

The search results show Dickey's Barbecue locations in the greater Palm Springs/Palm Desert area have generally positive customer feedback focusing on quality barbecue, friendly service, and family-friendly atmosphere.

Here are the search results for Chick-fil-A Palm Desert I-10:

## Ratings

The restaurant has a 3.4 Star Rating from 164 reviewers according to Chamber of Commerce, though based on 638 reviews on another platform, the restaurant has received a rating of 3.9 stars. On DoorDash, the rating is 4.7 out of 5.

## Location & Hours

The restaurant is located at 73070 Dinah Shore Dr, Palm Desert, CA 92211. Hours are Tuesday through Thursday 6:30 AM - 10:00 PM, Friday and Saturday 6:30 AM - 11:00 PM, Sunday Closed, and Monday 6:30 AM - 10:00 PM.

## Customer Reviews Summary

Reviews are mixed. Some reviewers praise it as "a newer Chick-fil-A" with "incredibly kind" owners and "super helpful and friendly" staff. However, others note some operational challenges, with wait times of 15 minutes or longer for food during busy periods and occasional service inconsistencies mentioned in some reviews.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Pokehana in Palm Desert on Yelp:

## Overall Rating and Reviews

Pokehana has a 4.4-star rating with 613 reviews, and customers praise it saying "Glad we found Pokehana. Delicious fresh poke!"

## What Makes It Stand Out

Pokéhana defines the concept of fresh craft poké created in a "fast casual" atmosphere. The restaurant has a health score of 100 out of 100, offers delivery and takeout, and takes reservations.

The restaurant is committed to serving the finest quality fish and fresh ingredients, with everything in each dish being house made and fresh-caught, with no bottled sauces or re-labeled ingredients.

## Customer Experience

Customers spend time talking to staff about each of the toppings they can choose from, which is described as fascinating to hear about their creative and tasty ingredients.

The establishment is described as trendy and casual, good for groups and kids, with categories including Sushi, Poke Bowl, Takeout, Salad, Lunch, and Noodles.

You can view the full Yelp listing at their official page for more detailed reviews and current information.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Monterey Crossing in Palm Desert:

## Project Overview
Monterey Crossings is a new retail center under construction at the northeast corner of Dinah Shore Drive and Monterey Avenue, with an 18-acre project that includes a 130-room, four-story hotel along with 130,000 square feet of single-story commercial space.

## Restaurant Tenants
Signed tenants include Chick-fil-A, Dickey's Barbecue Pit, and Pokehana. Additionally, the retail pad features Jersey Mike's Subs, Nekter Juice Bar, and Roll-Em-Up Taquitos. The Habit Burger Grill Drive-Thru is also located at Monterey Crossing.

## Other Notable Tenants
Beyond restaurants, major current tenants include Wal-Mart Supercenter, Sam's Club, Kohl's, Ashley Furniture, PetSmart, and numerous smaller retailers.

## Hotel Status
A 130-room, four-story hotel is still in the works for the site.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fast food options in north Palm Desert near I-10 and Monterey:

## Recommended Fast Food Options

**Burger King** - Located at 34500 Monterey Ave, this is conveniently positioned in the Monterey area.

**Jersey Mike's** - Located on Monterey off the I-10, this store is known for staff that takes pride in customer service.

**Del Taco** - There's a Del Taco on Monterey that serves tacos and burritos.

**Carl's Jr.** - Located on Hwy 111, this location has been praised for excellent, friendly, and professional service.

## Other Popular Fast Food Chains in Palm Desert

Popular fast food options in Palm Desert include In-N-Out Burger, Chick-fil-A, Raising Cane's Chicken Fingers, Del Taco, McDonald's, and Chipotle Mexican Grill.

The Monterey Avenue and Highway 111 corridor near I-10 appears to have good concentration of fast food options for quick, convenient meals.

content.text: I have enough information. This is a development cluster (shopping center) with three chain tenants. Let me check Pokehana since it's the local-chain element here.

input.prompt: Is there a Palm Desert location? What information is on the site about locations, hours, menu, and schema markup?

content.content:
# Pokehana Location Information

**Palm Desert Location:**
Yes, there is a Palm Desert location. The site states: "Pokehana- Palm Desert, 73405 CA-111 STE 104 PALM DESERT, CA 92260, (442) 274-8181"

**Hours:**
Both locations operate with identical hours: "11:00 am - 8:30 pm" daily, seven days per week.

**Menus:**
The site references menu options for both locations but displays them as buttons without providing visible menu details in the content shown.

**Online Ordering:**
Both locations offer online ordering through Clover's platform.

**Schema Markup:**
No structured schema markup is evident in the provided content. The page lacks JSON-LD or other semantic markup for business information, hours, or location data that would help search engines understand the restaurant details.

content.text: Note: The existing Pokehana listed is on Hwy 111, not at Monterey Crossings/Dinah Shore. The "Monterey Crossings Pokehana" reference may be a planned/separate location. The development cluster is the entity here. Let me finalize.

input.name: Monterey Crossings (Chick-fil-A, Dickey's Barbecue Pit, Pokehana)
input.cuisine_or_type: Fast-casual / chain dining cluster (anchor retail development)

reputation.review_volume: Cluster-level: Chick-fil-A ~210 Yelp reviews / ~638 Google reviews; Pokehana (Hwy 111 PD location, not Monterey Crossings pad) 229 Yelp reviews; Dickey's Palm Desert location reviews limited/dispersed [UNVERIFIED whether a Dickey's pad operates specifically at Monterey Crossings vs. nearby corridor]
reputation.sentiment: Mixed to mostly positive — Chick-fil-A 3.4-3.9 stars (mixed: service inconsistency, long waits flagged); Pokehana 4.4 stars (positive on freshness and service); Dickey's generally positive in regional listings
reputation.longevity: Development cluster: Chick-fil-A opened July 14, 2022; Monterey Crossings specific plan approved May 2017; cluster still building out with 130-room hotel pending [UNVERIFIED exact opening dates for Dickey's and Pokehana pads at Monterey Crossings specifically]
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (2025-2026) reviews note operational scale-up issues at the Chick-fil-A drive-thru during peak hours; Pokehana reviews continue to praise freshness and ingredient transparency; development continues to add tenants (Jersey Mike's, Nekter, Roll-Em-Up Taquitos, Habit Burger reported)

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Individual chain tenants surface for branded queries ('Chick-fil-A Palm Desert', 'Pokehana Palm Desert', 'Dickey's Palm Springs area'). The cluster name 'Monterey Crossings' surfaces in commercial real estate queries (LoopNet, Hanley Investment, KDC Construction) and trade press (Palm Springs Life Vision, GPS Business Insider) — not in consumer dining queries. Does NOT surface for 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Desert', 'date night Rancho Mirage'. May surface for 'fast food near I-10 Palm Desert' but as individual chain pins, not as a cluster.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When the development surfaces, descriptions are accurate to its commercial-real-estate framing (18-acre, 130,000 sq ft, NEC Dinah Shore & Monterey, Marriott TownePlace pending). Individual chain descriptions inherit corporate-brand accuracy. No conflation issues observed.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Monterey Crossings has no consumer-facing site — only developer/leasing pages (KDC Construction, Hanley Investment, LoopNet) and adwarchitects portfolio page. Tenant sites: Chick-fil-A and Dickey's run on robust corporate domains with location structured data. Pokehana's site (pokehana.com) lacks visible JSON-LD/schema.org markup; hours and locations are present but not machine-structured.

competitors: Westfield Palm Desert / Highway 111 fast-casual corridor
La Quinta Pavilion / Hwy 111 La Quinta retail
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — defines the north Palm Desert development zone (Dinah Shore/Monterey/I-10 gateway). Functions as freeway-adjacent commuter and traveler catchment rather than destination dining.
query_wins: commercial real estate / leasing queries for Palm Desert pads
Pokehana Palm Desert (branded, though primary pad is on Hwy 111)
north Palm Desert / Dinah Shore retail development press

convertibility.ownership_signal: chain (Chick-fil-A franchise, Dickey's franchise, Pokehana small regional operator); development owned/leased through commercial RE — Hanley Investment Group, KDC Construction. No reachable single local DM for the cluster.
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Cluster-level: developer/leasing marketing through Hanley, LoopNet, KDC. Tenant-level: corporate national spend for Chick-fil-A and Dickey's; Pokehana has a basic Clover-backed site with no visible agency footprint or schema markup. No evidence of cluster-level consumer marketing.
convertibility.reasoning: This is an anchor-reference entity, not a convertible client. Monterey Crossings is a commercial real estate development whose dining tenants are national/regional chains (Chick-fil-A, Dickey's, Pokehana) — there is no independently owned local restaurant here with a reachable decision-maker or budget for visibility services. The cluster's role in an AICV category map is as a north Palm Desert corridor anchor: it shapes the freeway-gateway food landscape, defines where chain-quality competition sits, and gives the map a reference point for nearby independent operators. Pokehana is the closest thing to a small operator (two-location regional), but its Monterey Crossings presence isn't confirmed at the canonical pokehana.com locations page (only Hwy 111 PD and La Quinta listed).

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Whether Pokehana operates a physical pad at Monterey Crossings (Dinah Shore) — canonical pokehana.com lists only the Hwy 111 PD and La Quinta locations; trade press lists Pokehana as a 'signed tenant' which may be planned/announced rather than open
[UNVERIFIED] Specific opening dates for Dickey's Barbecue Pit at Monterey Crossings pad versus other Coachella Valley Dickey's locations (Palm Springs Ramon Rd is the well-documented one)
[UNVERIFIED] Current operational status of the planned 130-room Marriott TownePlace Suites — reported as still in planning/works
Entity is a development cluster, not a single restaurant — review/sentiment metrics are tenant-level rollups, not unified
No third-party dining credentials (James Beard, Michelin, Desert Sun, Palm Springs Life dining awards) — none expected for chain anchors and none invented
